You born in to a Bedouin tribe, which was recruited by T.E. Lawrence, the idea of a homeland of your own was all you needed to run in to the spray of bullets. After Lawrence left Aquaba, and the troops stayed pillaging the city, you found a covered basement that was ignored by your brethren. Walking down there you found yourself transported to a different time, sneaking between the pillars you saw a man conversing with a djinn. So mesmerized you just walked out of your hiding place and stat next to him. Over the next 10 years, he taught you how to connect to the teachings of Islam in ways that always seemed like fairy tales. The idea of you becoming a vampire was just another of the wonders you became used to.
Things were good, your clan needed you, and you had allies in the Courts of the Ashirra it seemed things would continue this way until Cain’s rising. That all changed the night that Ur-Shulgi rose. The faiths of Christianity and Islam where banned, For once you could not obey. You had seen too much bartered with angels and demons, you did not have faith you knew. A few believed as you did, and decided that the enemy of my enemy is my friend. The Camarilla accepted you and your brothers, knowing your wisdom was going to be vital in the days ahead. 
While you cannot beat Ur-Shulgi’s physical power, you will win the war of ideas, you will make the Assamites in to a clan of respect and adoration that wields the weapons of faith and blood in equal measure. You have come to LA in search of an artifact, this necklace is a fetish of an angel you once knew named Wiehael, your divinations tell you it is here, and the owner does not know of it’s power. Convincing someone to give you an empowering artifact will take friends and patience.

#1 April Ueltschi (Ventrue) You have worked for her multiple times, but she never knew your identity, because you worked though intermediaries. She is a researcher from turkey, and you feel if any one will support you out of your clan she will. #2 Eugune Ducheski (Tremere) Your revenant contacts have told you of the ducheski line, if he has influence with revenants he is a threat to be acquired or crippled. #3 Focalor (Spirit Not In Game) You can take a downtime action to try to learn more about spirit goings in the city with this contact.

#1. Gather boons to later use to find and acquire the holding place of Wiehael. #2. Convert someone to your faith, who could deny believing when you have the power of faith with you. #3. Make sure your new Camarilla brothers do not treat your people poorly. Make sure that you have the proper titles, representation, embrace and domain rights of other clans. You are not a minor clan in importance and you should not be marginalized.
Brian Haney CS, EH, K, P

You would never have said you were good at shooting a gun. It was more the bullets just happened to go where you would need them the best, killing people is just what the result happened to be. You were just a ranch hand until a drunk called you out, and you shot him and his three friends before any of them could even shoot. The legend began to grow, and soon the only people you would meet where people who wanted to be near that fame, or wanted to challenge it. As it was luck and not skill, you got cocky until the dark stranger came, He had a skin unlike anyone had before, like a blackie but not almost like coal. He sad he wanted to see your speed and he was willing to put his life on the line, But it had to be at night. You agreed, and then went off to get drunk, after all it did not take skill. That night when you fought him you saw him dodge every one of your shots, and after your guns were empty he shot 2 shots one in each of your palms. Dragging you off in to the night he taught you respect, patience and the importance of training over just luck. You adapted to vampire life, and after a decade you started taking contacts in the camarilla courts of America. You kept a low profile, but a good reputation. About 10 years ago your sire found you after you had cleared out a Sabbat back out of Miami, and told you that the father clan had betrayed you both. The camarilla was no longer just our employer they are now our home. He took you to meet some of the other new Assamite Cam members, and they took you in as a brother. Now that you belong to a sect, and your clan has a justicar, you want to become known again. The lure of fame, has bit you as you have exited the shadows, maybe you could become a sheriff, and then maybe just maybe an Archon after all you are the best, now with luck and training.

#1 Laura Alvarado (Toreador) You worked on her fathers farm iny your mortal days, She does not remember you, but she has a face you could never forget. #2 Marco Del Juro (Ventrue) A famous gunsmith and weapons dealer in the supernatural circles, you have waned a Del Juro gun for years, and the idea of meeting him almost makes your heart beat.  #3 Marco Enrathi (Revenant Not In Game) Your travels have had you meet this weird man, he has the powers of a vampire but is alive.. He said he live near Los Angeles if you ever needed help.

#1. Prove your worth, though tests and trials. No one can beat your skill now. #2. Try to make contacts with the Archons, to start in roads for you to become one as well. #3. Now that your in the Camarilla, never let them forget you could just as easily leave, if they treat you poorly.

You were never a good person, taking what you wanted was just the way things were just the way things were. When you were pressed in to a pirate ship you did not resist, you had found your brothers. Your skill with the blade increased, as your reputation did as well. One night when on watch you saw a boat drifting in the sea. Silently sailing to it, you boarded it, looking for booty. The moment one of the crew went below decks hell rose out of them. Vampires burst out drinking their full and converting the others, their ritual to convert was to strap a newly embraced member to the anchor, and wait an hour for their humanity to be bled out of them. The new vampires were sent to attack ship after ship of the camarilla, your bothers would fall 10 in one blow, but not you. You quickly learned your new skills and after 2 months there was only 3 of you left. You were brought in to the Sabbat though the rituals of the blood pact as a reward before the ship took port. While in a moment you loved your brothers you also feared them. 
As the ship was tied to the mooring, you jumped and ran. It feels like you have never stopped. If you are caught you will be killed, and that fear wakes you in blood sweat every night. For protection you have decided to live with the foes of those who hunt you. Killing a less experienced member of the Assamites, you took his identify as your new sire, learning the laws and trying to become too valuable to be disbelieved.  You had made a place for yourself in the courts of Dubai, sometimes as an enforcer, some time as just a simple problem solver. That was until the night that you saw one of the men from the ship walking the streets, that night you got on a plane to Los Angeles, hoping that the other side of the world would be far enough to run.

#1 Scott Davenport (Toreador) An expert martial artist who’s movies you have loved for years. #2 April Ueltshci (Ventrue) You met her in the court of Istanbul, while you where running, even though you have only met twice, she has a bewitching look that you have never forgotten. #3 Heifa Quan (NPC Seneschal, of Dubai) A good source of advice and information, keeping up good relations is vital to keep your cover safe.

#1. Find a way to clear yourself of blood ritual of the Sabbat maybe the full bond to a powerful vampire can over come it. #2. Find some way to change your physical form, so you can hide better.  #3. Make such a place for yourself in los angeles, that no one looks in to your past.
Ragin Branch CS, EH, K, P

As a young child you loved the game of hide and seek. The thrill of being hunted and turning the tables with you being the hunter satisfied you more then anything. It did not take long for your homeland to become a warzone, and as a Croats you took up arms, your faith in Allah and faith of your father and your fathers father had to be preserved. You took your game of hide and seek to another level and it became all the more addictive when death was on the line. By luck or patience you got better, and better. Some might say too good, your reputation grew to the point that you were no longer being hunted just by humans. One night you found your snipers nest, and looked down the sight. Only to be shocked as there was another person in your nest with you. You talked the night away, he told you of powers to hide from men’s minds just by thinking, the skill of centuries at your grasp. All you had to do was follow him.
He took you to Lebanon deep in the desert, Were you trained with your fellow recruits, many also who where of the faith.  You were trained hard for 10 years, and then brought in to the Assamites. You took your contacts, the blood tithe all of it was more and more of the childish game. Then everything changed; your sire told you that we were now all criminals, the father clan hunted you now.
Contacting some of the people who you had done missions for in the past, you found a place in the Camarilla in New Orleans. You felt safe for a moment; you had a group of brothers with you, and got lazy. 
In a matter of 3 nights, the Children of Haqim came down on your people, 2 the first died the first night, killed in a flash. 4 the next, you stood with your brothers until you were alone. Your childish instincts kicked in. You hid. You saw them examine the bodies, and you ran. You ran for a few years, then came the call to come to Los Angels, there are other Assamites here you must protect.

#1 Mary Fay Satterfield (Brujha) A southern bell that you had heard of in the courts of New Orleans. #2 Frenando Mosqueda (Nosferantu) He is a human smuggler you have heard of, he might help getting more of your clan safely to the city. #3 Nicko Maroocs (NPC Assamite) Your sire has connections with others of our kine, he tries to keep things safe, any help you can give him will be returned in kind.

You learned more about yourself, that first time you gave half of your hamburger to a stray dog, the harmony of hearing the loons at night. You were always the girl that no, was never an option. Just look pretty and find a man was an insult to your abilities. You got your masters in chemistry, on a sports scholarship. When you went home after graduating, your parents just kept bringing men over to try to get you married. You were not opposed to the idea of guys or marriage, but you could not get the idea that you were not good enough out of your head. So you moved to the first job you could get out of your town, you worked in the Medical Examiners office from there, you kept pushing yourself, from that to the FBI, to SWAT training to being a leader in your field. 
With all your cases there was one that you could not just let be. A bloody ashy human outline with no accelerants found at all. You knew this was the case that if it broke would take you to the next level. As you where looking though some of the evidence, a shooter, walked in and sprayed the room with autopistol fire. You passed out as the bullets passed though your body. You woke up in the ICU, with only one man sitting on a chair next to you. He explained to you how this was the final promotion. You had the true ability to control your destiny.  He would teach you much in the next few weeks, the Camarilla, the nature of you new form, how to thrive. Then he said he would see you again in 11 years in Los Angeles.  You have not wasted your time, you have become a valuable member of the Camarilla. It bothers you that your clan is not given equal ranking while you can’t fix it world wide maybe you can fix it in your sphere of influence. You came to LA early waiting for your sire to get in contact with you this November.

#1 Alexander Baird (Brujah) He has worked in a lot of courts, but seems cursed some how to never thrive.. #2 Nathaniel Johnson (Ventrue) A crime boss you had been looking for in your mortal days, finding him in the vampire world is not surprising. #3 Frank Baker (NPC) This is your contact at the LA FBI office, he has been of help in some cases but you think there is something secret about him as well.

#1. Try to get representation for the gangrel, though the proof of your abilities. #2. Prepare for your sires return; make sure he has an acknowledgement. #3. Learn about the enemies of the Camarilla in this city.

You were a strong member of your Tribe, You raided well, and enjoyed the simple joys of hearth and home. But on one raid you were distracted, and a farmers pitchfork pierced your leg. It seemed like a simple thing but the festering grew and you soon became delirious. You made it back home, and your kin took you to the ancient shaman who knew the mysteries of the land and the sea. Your family failed you, the doorway to Valhalla was there, and the sorcerer pulled you back.  His potions, made you in to a shadowalker, unable to bask in the light. Over the centuries he told you of his people, blessed by a kinslayer. That his special powers came from a book, ancient even when he was born. He never let you see it inside but you will never forget it’s cover. 
Over time you yearned for lands beyond, and it seemed everywhere people had need for a strong back and a sharp blade, the crusades, the horde, the age of pirates, the conquering of the American west all wars that you have made your mark. You would learn and forget tonghes in a matter of months. Everywhere people are the same. The only one different was the sorcerer who pulled you in. He had the spark of life in him while the powers of death, no other shadowalker has had his smell. 
One night you awoke from torpor and there was a group of campers, watching a TV, you spied from the woods waiting for your moment to strike. Then you saw it. On the screen was a documentary showing the book of your sire. How it has never been translated but it is dated to the 4th century. But more importantly it is currently held in a secure exhibit of the Getty Museum in Los Angeles. As this has become less a time of warriors, you know you need help to acquire it, and translate it.

#1 Aarif Al-Wasdea (Assamite) A Bedouin warrior you met during the great war. You saved his life, durring the battle of Aqubua. #2 Isha Parikh (Tremere) A famous translator, if anyone in los angeles can find an answer to the book she can. #3 Albert Voss (NPC head curator over the book) He as spent his whole life learning about the book, but you will have to find a way to talk to him without him knowing of your vampirism.

#1. Acquire and get the book translated. After you have the book gain an apprentice like you were to Maku, maybe that is needed to unlock its secrets. #2. Your people seem mistreated here try to aid them as best as you can. #3. Try learn a needed talent other then just killing. Mankind finally has made peace in their homes, change with these new times.

You and your twin brother (Jake) after the war could not stop the desire to keep moving, for a while bikes kept you two excided, but that did not pay the bills. Then you started shipping drugs for some biker gangs, and that moved it to being full on smugglers. Traveling with your brother never seemed lonely and you even did some normal shipping if the price was right. One night you went to go get some groceries, and you where attacked. You were made in to a vampire that night, your sire kept you locked in a cave when he taught you the basics. In a week, though your brother found you. He walked in as your sire was trying to teach you about ghouling. Your bother put the end of a double barrel shotgun, on your sire’s head and fired. You two ran for years, Hiding from other vampires, finding your blood where you could. You kept him ghouled so he would never age, and he would act as you during the day. Things were going well until, one day you wanted to deliver a package but the person never showed up. I was born to a fairly well-to-do family in 1865, in New York City. My childhood was a nightmare. I could not understand the compulsions I had nor could I ignore them. I had to count my steps. I had to arrange things just so. As I grew older, it got worse and worse. My parents, in their ignorance, tied me to my bed and shot me full of drugs. An endless parade of physicians threw up their hands in frustration. Eventually, even my parents gave up and I was committed to an asylum.  The thought that removal of stimulus would help, but I still paced my cell and rearranged my food. Eventually, he found me. Dr. Thomas Everett, whom I was told as a famous physician, was coming to study my case. He was warm and charming. When he looked at me, I could help feel as though my soul was transparent before him. He reversed my sensory deprivation and gave me books, ink and paper. He told me to read and write down everything I wanted. Free of my family’s fearful looks, and all the time in the world to delve into my obsessions, I unwittingly became his master pupil. I learned languages, legends, secrets. I was able to see patterns and connections far beyond the pompous notes of historical scholars. And when I proved myself a master pupil, he embraced me in the year of our Lord: 1888. The decades that followed were a whirlwind of research and travel. I’ve walked between the Tigris and Euphrates. I’ve seen the pyramids at midnight. I’ve breathed in the scent of ancient tomes. The story of the Cainite race, or “Kindred” as we are supposed to call ourselves, is like a black thread that has been woven into the tapestry of human existence. We have always been there, intertwined but difficult to notice. So much has been forgotten, and I was determined to discover it all. Noddist lore was declared heresy, my sire went to ground. Though I was accused, I was sufficiently insolated by his machinations, to weather the storm. Though he asked me to discontinue my own research, I knew I could not. The truth must be found. The truth must be known to all. I will find it, and I will show them all.

Growing up in Chicago in the late 70’s, was like being in the middle of a musical multi-car pile-up. The carcass of blues based, formulaic family tree was rotting away. The responsibility rejecting, coke fueled dance bunnies were getting their Disco snorts. It wasn’t until the likes of Ultravox, Depeche Mode, and the glory of the singing machines came into the limelight that I truly gave a shit. More than that; it was an awakening. I was all in. Rubber bracelets, fishnets, teased hair. It was my scene. As a child I had a natural affinity for piano. My ear was so good, I could play along to cartoon themes even before I understood what I was doing. But electronics, man. Now you didn’t just hit a note. You could bend it, stretch it, garble it up or make it sound truly alien. It set my creative mind on afterburner and I cranked out masterpiece after masterpiece. It spoke to me. Not in the way some poser with a paintbrush says the Mona Lisa speaks to him. The trills and sweeps, they spoke to me; deep in my brain I saw threads that interlocked with others. I heard a muffled cacophony of voices speaking… history? I didn’t really understand then. And no, it wasn’t acid.By the early 90’s, I was DJing underground raves and passing out tape mixes of my stuff. It was a never-ending dance party bus, and I was the driver. One August night in 1994, in some warehouse outside of Cleveland, I made out with the weirdest guy. No one ever talked about my music like he did. No one aside from me. I was 24 and thought I knew everything, but he understood my music on a level that even I didn’t. He embraced me that night and introduced me to a new, terrifying depth of understanding. The things I used to see in the music, the voices; they are all clear as day now. The only “day” I’ll ever see. The music is still the core of my being. And now the L.A. is open territory, of course I had to go. It’s Mecca to the music monsters, right?

In 1966 Timothy Leary told us to “Turn on, tune in, drop out”. But I wasn’t some lost college kid. I had degrees in chemistry and psychology and was empowered by the free thinking of the era. So perhaps I couldn’t drop out, but I did turn on and tune in. I applied science to the philosophy that mind-expanding substances could elevate human consciousness. After all, shamans had been following the practice for thousands of years. They key was finding the right one. Despite the fringe nature of my research, I nonetheless found funding at the hands of Leary’s associates and a mystery benefactor. The key to “ascension”, as I like call it, eluded me as my friends and patrons began to straighten up, fleeing the country, or dying of excess. I myself was on the brink of breaking as my obsession became desperation. That is when my mystery benefactor found me and bestowed upon me true ascension.Julius Windham, my sire, shattered the limiting lenses I had over my eyes. While drugs showed me the chaos subsumed beneath the ordinary, the embrace gave means to see the patterns within. The chaos could be understood and read like a book. I was overjoyed at this gift and pledged to share it with the world. But you cannot populate a world with Kindred. That in impractical. Moreover, Kindred are flawed in their own way. I will find the path to the ascension of the human race. The secret is in my blood, and it will reveal itself to me in time. My research inexplicably unnerves my fellow Kindred. I find myself needing to move often. I don’t understand it, but I bare them no ill will. Perhaps my brethren in Los Angeles will be more understanding.

Going forward, I wasn’t commanded by my officers, I was unleashed; for I had no regard for life (mine or otherwise). I would stalk the enemy trenches. They didn’t expect a single lunatic to bring death to their doorstep. I reveled in it. This slaughter was not for Britannia, it was sacrifice for something darker within me. It was on one of these excursions that I came across a man with eyes as wild as my own. He did not shrink from me as the Germans instinctively would do. He leapt at me and with supernatural strength, dragged me to a nearby cave. For the rest of that night, he taught me what a monster truly was. He cut me, bled me. He showed me the Beast, before I could fully comprehend. Finally, he embraced me. It was the only way I could grasp the depths of my depravity–to see the monster I’d become. Once again, I found myself the sword of nameless masters. When I would teeter over the edge of the abyss, my sire did just enough to pull me back. It finally dawned on me that I would never be anything more than a monster, if I didn’t go off on my own. So I left my sire in search of this Golconda I’d heard rumors over. For decades I have travelled the world in search of knowledge and teachers. While I have had some success, I still have a long road to travel. I have come to Los Angeles in search of scholars and lore. Perhaps I will find what I need to take another step forward on the road to Golconda.

During the depression, Boston was the kind of town that could eat you alive. I was a young reporter trying to cover the struggle of everyday people; to bring awareness to the suffering those folks in the ivory towers had grown numb to. I don’t know, maybe I’d grown numb to it too. However many starving kids and weeping mothers can you see before something inside you dies? The boss thought that what we really needed was a new perspective. maybe if we reported on the most horrific acts of depravity, the average Joe wouldn’t think his life so bad. I didn’t buy it, but it was my job and I needed to keep it. The problem was that there were too many guys scouring the city for stories, and not enough them to go around. When he threatened to fire me, I decided to take matter into my own hands. I didn’t really have the luxury of starting slow. I decided to try robbing a department store at gunpoint. Of course it ended badly. The only reason I lived was because I pulled the trigger first. As I ran, cash in hand and adrenaline pumping through my veins, I realized that I was exhilarated. Better still, I had a bag of cash and a story to tell. It was all downhill from there. Depravity came as easy to me as typing words on a page. And who would suspect a reporter? One fateful night in 1935, it all came to an end. No, I didn’t have a sudden flash of conscience. I didn’t see the error of my ways. Quite simply, I picked on the wrong lady. She knew. She looked into my heart and knew. That night she created a story of her own, doing things to me that I could never think of. I remember realizing just how much of an amateur I was, as she drained the blood from my body. She wasn’t about to let me die. It was a good thing, in the end. My “gory days” gave me plenty of stories to tell. I sold a bunch of novels thanks to them and no one ever caught on that the things I described were true. That I’d done them with my own hands. Maybe they knew deep down. I mean how can a virgin truly write about making love? L.A.’s a new scene. I’ve had that itch to hunt for stories again. The City of Angels is a pretty grim place; maybe I won’t have to make my own stories this time. That’d be a shame.

The world  is a frightening place. As a child, I would tremble as I walked down the streets. The buildings loomed over me like towering monsters. The people on the streets stalked me like spiders. I feared the sun would burn me. I feared the night would take me. All day; everyday, I lived in fear. My family was wealthy enough to afford my special needs. They were also callous enough to lock me away in institutions whenever I got really bad. By the time I was 16, I was convinced that my only escape from the fear and isolation was death. Three times I tried to take my life. Each time I returned from the abyss with less fear. After the third, I became fear. Anyone that looked into my eyes saw the nothingness inside me. Even my cruel family walked on eggshells around me. They were right to. My thoughts turned from fearing monsters to becoming one. The only way to conquer my fear was to become fear itself. It started small – my siblings started finding their pets mangled in their beds. By the time I was 19, I would dress as innocent and wholesome as I could and wander the streets at night. I would attract the monsters, then turn the tables on them. My first was a would-be rapist whom I left without eyes. The next, a mugger who’s screams I stole with his tongue. Many followed and each made the same mistake of underestimating me. They thought me the victim. I never killed, though. The dead do not know fear. One night, I attracted a different kind of monster. I don’t know if it was coincidence or if Sal was waiting for me, but that night I realized I still had much to learn. Sal and I came to L.A. to explore new ground. I reprised my favorite role as the innocent victim, wandering my new surroundings. This time, I did not find a new kind of monster. I found something familiar: fear. I found brood of wretched souls hiding in the night. They are Kindred but still very human. They are hunted. Killed off like vermin. Victims one and all. These must be the “Thinbloods” that are spoken about in whispers. These pathetic creatures inspire fear in the hearts of elders. They are just children. Fear’s children.

It was the golden age of Hollywood, and I was the new rising star. I was a young, good looking actor rubbing elbows with the elite and surrounded by the sweetest angels of the city. Not that I didn’t deserve it. I studied and suffered. I honed my craft and made myself great. And as much as I loved the scene and the lifestyle, I felt truly fulfilled and alive on a movie set. In those days, every shark in the city wanted to sink their teeth into me. I avoided them all… all but one. Danica was the spider at the center of the web. She had her fingers in every pie in Hollywood and had been quietly guiding my carrier from afar. She spoke of my talent my beauty like you’d talk about a withering rose. It would bring her to tears-the thought that someday I’d grow old, die and my talent lost forever. So that night she made sure that I’d never wither away; and on a rainy November night in 1926, I was embraced. The price for my newfound immortality was great. I could never be a star again. My final, classic performance would be the original Hollywood cliché; to be the young star that would burn out too soon. Still, I managed to find ways to remain in the industry I loved so much. I’ve played the part of director, producer, financier, agent and the occasional extra in the background. Decades later, I find myself questioning all that has happened. My entire life and unlife reads like a script. Maybe I am just a player in someone else’s tour de force. And though my sire denies that we are being manipulated or even inspired by something… bigger than ourselves, her eyes tell a different story.

Some producer who’d followed my career offered to hook me up with some people in Hong Kong to try my hand at martial arts movies. It ended up being some of the best years of my life. Now in my early 30’s, and living well, life dropped another bombshell on me when I was diagnosed Cancer. This time, I couldn’t pick myself up. I went into a suicidal binge of every vice imaginable. Ever since I was little, I loved to draw. I would doodle on everything. Honestly though, I was never good at it. The picture in my mind never really translated well through my hands and onto paper. I did, however, have a knack for computers and programming. Naturally my parents and teachers encouraged me to put down the sketchbook and take up the keyboard instead. I can’t really say it was a bad call. I excelled tremendously and had recruiters beating down my door even before I finished college. Still, I never lost my passion for drawing, so I decided to put my programming skills to work, and create software that could help anyone become the artist they dream to be. Digital Artist” revolutionized the art industry. One piece of software could replace thousands of dollars in art supplies and equipment. The digital artist was born. Several updates and editions followed, making me a wealthy man. I was considered the founding father of a new era in art. The old guard hated me for it. I didn’t set out to start a revolution, but I was at the center of one now. The president of my distribution partner introduced me to Ilene Bennett; a beautiful business woman who donated heavily to the arts and was a well known patron of up and coming artists. We shared a passion for change, progress and shared a mutual respect for one another. I invited her over for a drink and our conversation carried on into the night. That night she offered me immortality and an opportunity to breathe new life into the “Clan of the Rose”. What can I say? She was very convincing, and I love a good challenge. The scorn and resistance I received from the mortal art world was nothing compared to the old fossils of Clan Toreador. At least those old men had wardrobes that belonged to this century! Though they don’t understand what I’ve created, nor do they respect my talent; they fear me because I am the writing on the wall. The world is changing. Art is changing. I grew up the faithful daughter of wealth Spanish land owner in Sonoma, California. And though I was educated and quite opinionated, I never would have thought to go against my father’s wishes. Not until I met Alexi. We made an odd couple: a fur trader and a senorita. But we were happy and in love, and I with child. It was inevitable that my father would discover us. The sweet man I’d known my whole life became a cruel and murderous monster. He beat me til I miscarried. He had my beloved executed on false charges. Whatever lingering love I had for my father, died along with Alexi. It was all to avoid scandal, to protect the family name. Somehow it made it worse knowing that was business instead of personal. He took everything I loved from me, so I vowed to do the same to him. Stealing his life away was easy. Even though he knew how much I hated him, he could never imagine that his little girl would lift a finger against him. Over years I slowly poisoned him, trying to learn all I could about the family holdings and business as he grew sick and weak. I listened at dinner parties, eavesdropped on his conversations, and read every book he read. When death finally came, it was only natural that I take over the business, I was the eldest child and my mother never took it upon herself to learn. My machinations did not go unnoticed however, and I was soon confronted by the man that would become my sire. No other soul save for Alexi, had appreciated my intellect and actions such as he. He taught me how to be a Kindred and Toreador; and in short order, I was once again my own woman. For nearly two centuries I have been a steady influence in California. Through Anarch revolutions and Camarilla reconquista, I have remained. Now I come to the City of Angels to take might rightful place amongst the elite. After all, it is my birth and blood right.

Growing up, I always looked to writing to escape my boredom. Through high school and college I impressed my instructors, and it wasn’t too long after I graduated that I published my first novel. It certainly wouldn’t be the last. Success came quick and I found myself scrambling to remain anonymous. I didn’t want the fame, I just wanted my works to be appreciated by the masses. And though I became very adept at keeping my true identity hidden, the woman that would become my sire managed to find me. Though she was beautiful, her obsession with my work frightened me. By the time she offered to “preserve” my gift forever, I was in a near panic. She embraced me that night. At first, I was delighted. I would have eternity to hone my craft. It quickly became evident however, that I’d lost some essential part of me when I lost my mortal life. That creative spark that every artist thrives upon  was simply gone. Every time I sat at my typewriter words would flow forth, but it was nothing more than poorly veiled regurgitations of my previous works. Each night since has been a torture. Desperate to regain my sparks, I travel far and wide. I’ve seek out the wisest of us and delve into the darker regions of Kindred society. I’m at my wits end. My spark keeps eluding me, and despite the eternity before me, I find my existence has no meaning or purpose. Not too long after arriving in L.A., a man in my employ suggested I visit a place that has been “giving hope to many that have lost much”. It was a place called “Mistress Isia’s Temple of Secrets”. I went, of course, and discovered it to be some kind of underground church; the kind that takes your life savings for a few morsels of self-help “wisdom”. As I listened and watched, I realized there was something more sinister going on. This was a full-fledged vampire worship cult! I made a discrete, hasty exit. Hopefully they did not notice me. Hopefully I am not in danger. Do I tell the authorities? Do I risk somehow being an accessory to this crime because I sought out the temple myself?

Poseur #3 Shari Rosenthal (Malkavian) you have heard she used to be a Scholar of vampire lore, maybe he can tell you more about vampire worship cults. #4 George Yumy ( NPC) he is the one who suggested you check the church out.

#1. Continue to research a way to regain your spark. Leave no rock unturned or avenue unexplored; no matter the risk. #2. Seek out other artists to find inspiration and build up your social clout. #3. Learn all you can about the “Temple of Secrets” without being discovered.
Matt Segal CS, EH, K, P

I’ve always been drawn to the elegance of crime. Any fool can rob a liquor store for a few c-notes and get caught. Frying the bigger fish and getting away scot-free? That takes skill and talent. You do what you have to-to survive; I got good at taking things from people that had more than I did. I wasn’t perfect. I spend the better part of my first 25 year in some sort of confinement. Each incarceration was a lesson though, and in order to survive, I got good at escaping. Nineteen times to be exact. I’d had enough of jail cells though and took up as honest work as I could stomach: assisting stage magicians. To my surprise, I enjoyed it. I went from assistant to apprentice and by the late 70’s, was carving out my own name. I started noticing a man at every one of my shows. He would sit in the same seat in the front row every time. He didn’t take in the show like a typical fan, he was studying me. For nearly a year he attended my shows; each time arriving just before the curtain went up, and leaving just as the curtain dropped. On September 16, 1978, he was waiting for me in my apartment. He revealed to me that he was a magician as well, but had seemingly lost his passion and found it difficult to embrace modern techniques. He needed someone to quicken his spirit and show him all that was new to the old game. He needed someone to live through vicariously. He embraced me that night. Very quickly my situation felt like another form of incarceration. He would analyze, criticize; but had nothing to offer that would help. As soon as I could, I found a way to escape. On my own, the old survival instincts kicked in. It’s amazing how my two skill-sets complement each other. I still keep in contact with the ol’ Burnout, but strictly from afar. Opposite coast far.
